string.text:
The Arisaka Type 99 is a bolt-action rifle that was the standard infantry rifle of the Imperial Japanese Army from 1939 till the end of World War II.
 Featuring a cock-on-closing action and an unusual safety mechanism, the Type 99 has one of the strongest actions ever made for a bolt action.
 During the Korean War, approximately 126,500 short and 6,650 long Type 99 rifles were re-chambered to fire the standard .30-06 Springfield cartridge.
 This specific model was converted to fire 7.92?57mm ammo for the National Revolutionary Army.

 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS: 
 %c[d_purple]• %c[ui_gray_2] PU scope

The Arisaka Type 99 is a bolt-action rifle that was the standard infantry rifle of the Imperial Japanese Army from 1939 till the end of World War II.
 Featuring a cock-on-closing action and an unusual safety mechanism, the Type 99 has one of the strongest actions ever made for a bolt action.
 During the Korean War, a number of Type 99 rifles were re-chambered to fire the standard .30-06 Springfield cartridge. Accuracy suffered, due to the difference in cartridges, rifling rate and characteristics, but they were nonetheless functional.
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS: 
 %c[d_purple]• %c[ui_gray_2] PU scope
